git.description: Git clone URL of the package repository (e.g. `https://github.com/dbt-labs/dbt_utils.git`).

revision.description: Revision to check out: a tag, branch, or commit SHA.

subdirectory.description: Subdirectory of the repo where the dbt package is located.

warn-unpinned.description: Suppress the warning emitted when `revision` is unpinned (i.e. a branch name like `main`).

install-prerelease.description: Allow installation of pre-release versions when resolving `version`.

package.description: Package identifier on the dbt package hub, in `org/name` form (e.g. `dbt-labs/dbt_utils`).

version.description: Version pin. Accepts a single version string, a list of constraints (e.g. `[">=1.0.0", "<2.0.0"]`), or a number.

local.description: Filesystem path to the local dbt package, relative to the project root.

private.description: Private package identifier. Two-segment `org/repo` for GitHub, GitLab, or Azure DevOps; three-or-more-segment `org/group/repo` for GitLab subgroups or Azure DevOps `org/project/repo` (`ado` / `azure_devops`) when you want to pin the project explicitly.

provider.description: Git provider. Defaults to `github` when omitted. Use `ado` for Azure DevOps — `azure_devops` and `azure_active_directory` are legacy aliases.

revision.description: Revision to check out: a tag, branch, or commit SHA.

subdirectory.description: Subdirectory of the repo where the dbt package is located.

warn-unpinned.description: Suppress the warning emitted when `revision` is unpinned (i.e. a branch name like `main`).

PrivatePackageProvider.description:
Git provider for a private package.

Use `ado` (canonical). `azure_devops` and `azure_active_directory` are legacy aliases accepted for backward compatibility — prefer `ado` for new configurations.

tarball.description: HTTPS URL of a `.tar.gz` archive containing the dbt package.
